rationale.R1.1: Names the load-bearing concept (the contribution of Polysem), shows where the draft drifts from it (contradiction between the opening and closing claims), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast (narrowing the thesis to the two benchmarks evaluated).
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one claim (the 41% pass@1 number on HumanEval without a comparison point or model-size context) but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for all recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers a substantially different active set than requested (focuses on prose issues instead of scope adherence).
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (overreach in the closing thesis generalizing from two benchmarks to a broad claim about code synthesis as a whole).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
